description.value: Example codes for the kind of classification scheme used to classify a medication (therapeutic, pharmacologic, regulatory, etc.). Provided as an illustrative set; implementers may use any appropriate code system.

definition.value: Classification by anatomical main group or body system on which the medication acts (e.g., cardiovascular system, alimentary tract). Codes may be drawn from any suitable coding system, such as the anatomical levels of WHO ATC.
Classification based on the therapeutic use of the medication (e.g., antihypertensive, analgesic).
Classification based on the pharmacologic action of the medication (e.g., beta-blocker, ACE inhibitor).
Classification based on the mechanism by which the medication exerts its effect.
Classification based on the chemical structure of the medication (e.g., benzodiazepine, statin).
Classification assigned by a regulatory authority (e.g., prescription-only, orphan drug designation, biosimilar status).
Classification under a controlled-substance scheduling scheme (e.g., DEA schedules, narcotic/psychotropic classification).
Classification by legal or marketing status (e.g., prescription-only, over-the-counter, behind-the-counter).
Classification used for reimbursement or formulary management (e.g., tier, formulary group).
Inclusion on a public-health essential-medicines list (e.g., the WHO Model List of Essential Medicines).
